compiled_state.title: Write RED tests for SC-4 and SC-2 guards
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: Extend tests/unit/scripts/test-apply-attribution-trailers.sh with: test_script_exits_0_without_modifying_commit_msg_when_jsonl_absent() (SC-4: rm JSONL, run script, test $? -eq 0 && diff commit.txt), test_script_exits_0_without_modifying_commit_msg_when_jsonl_empty() (SC-4: :> JSONL, same check), test_script_exits_0_without_modifying_commit_msg_when_attribution_disabled() (SC-2: write config with attribution.enabled=false + populated JSONL -> exit 0, file unchanged). Use config-file mock (temp dso-config.conf) not bare env var. Tests fail (RED). TDD exemption: test-writing task.

comments.body:
Gap Analysis AC amendment: Test setup must explicitly set attribution.enabled=true to exercise the SC-4 guard.

If the test sets attribution.enabled=false (or omits it), T3b-impl's config gate exits 0 before reaching the JSONL check — the test would pass trivially for the wrong reason, not because the SC-4 guard is implemented.

Amended test setup for all SC-4 tests: explicitly configure the mock read-config.sh to return 'true' when called with 'attribution.enabled', so the config gate passes and the JSONL guard is actually exercised. This ensures the RED test fails because the guard is absent (not because attribution is disabled).
comments.author: Test
